_title: NFPA 70 Article 680 — Swimming Pools, Fountains, and Similar Installations

articles.article_title: Swimming Pools, Fountains, and Similar Installations
articles.scope: Permanently installed pools (Part II — 680.20+), storable pools (Part III — 680.30+), spas/hot tubs (Part IV — 680.40+), fountains (Part V — 680.50+), hydromassage bathtubs (Part VII — 680.70+). GFCI rules, equipotential bonding (680.26), receptacle clearances (5 ft / 10 ft / 20 ft rules), underwater lighting.

key_sections.title: Motors — Permanently Installed Pools
key_sections.summary: Pool pump motor branch circuits: GFCI protection (680.21(C)) for single-phase 120-240 V

key_sections.title: Area Lighting, Receptacles, and Equipment
key_sections.summary: Receptacle outlets: at least one within 6-20 ft of pool wall (681.22(A)), all 125 V 15/20 A receptacles within 20 ft of pool wall GFCI-protected, NO receptacles within 5 ft of inside wall of pool except low-voltage installations

key_sections.title: Equipotential Bonding
key_sections.summary: Equipotential bonding grid required at all permanently installed pools — connects: pool reinforcing steel, deck steel/copper grid, metal pool fittings, pool water (via conductive surface or intentional grid), pumps, lights, fences within 5 ft, etc. #8 AWG solid copper minimum, 4500 lb tensile strength

key_sections.title: Spas and Hot Tubs (Outdoor)
key_sections.summary: GFCI for all 125 V receptacles within 10 ft of inside walls of spa/hot tub. Indoor spas: same rules + interior wiring per Part IV

key_sections.title: Fountains — General
key_sections.summary: Fountains include decorative pools, water displays, etc. Conductive metal parts equipotentially bonded; underwater lighting limited to specific listings

key_sections.title: Hydromassage Bathtub — Bonding
key_sections.summary: Hydromassage bathtub: all metal piping/parts and motor frame bonded together with #8 AWG solid copper

Pool_Receptacle_Distance_Rules_680.22.title: Receptacle Distances from Pool Inside Wall

rows.rule: No general-purpose receptacle (low-voltage equipment receptacle exception)
At least one general-purpose receptacle required. GFCI-protected
Receptacles permitted; GFCI required for those serving pool equipment

common_errors: Omitting the equipotential bonding grid (680.26) — most frequent pool inspection failure
Specifying a receptacle within 5 ft of pool inside wall — prohibited (except low-voltage)
Treating an outdoor hot tub like a regular outdoor receptacle — 680.42 GFCI distance is 10 ft, not the dwelling-outdoor 6 ft
Using #10 AWG for equipotential bonding — 680.26 requires #8 AWG solid copper
articles.divergence_notes: NEC 680.22 uses imperial distance rules (5 ft / 6-20 ft / 20 ft / 10 ft for spas). IEC 60364-7-702 uses zone classification (Zone 0 = pool water + 0.3 m around, Zone 1 = 2 m horizontal from pool edge, Zone 2 = 1.5 m beyond Zone 1) with different rules per zone. NEC equipotential bonding grid (680.26) ≈ IEC 60364-7-702 supplementary bonding but NEC is more prescriptive on grid construction (#8 AWG solid Cu).
articles.usage_notes: Coordinate with the pool contractor's bonding plan and the local AHJ — equipotential bonding is THE pool electrical safety requirement.
